trim_reasons: VOLATILE tag on a 3PM prop with iron_floor incompatibility already stripped means no floor protection — LaMelo's wide 3PM distribution (raw avg 4.4 but volatile) means a cold-shooting night easily produces a miss at T2, and the 75% confidence reflects a real structural gap rather than a padded floor.

min_floor of 23.9 is just below the 24-minute threshold for minutes fragility, and the post-miss bounce-back hit rate is only 60% (lift=0.73 negative), meaning Bridges has a documented pattern of sequential underperformance — a single low-usage game produces a miss at a tier the system already walked down to T10.

FG_COLD at -30% is a severe active efficiency flag — per historical lessons, FG_COLD on a PTS prop signals a structurally elevated same-game miss probability, and the confidence was already stepped down significantly; a player in active shooting cold spell at T10 is one more cold-FG night from a miss even at this conservative tier.

VOLATILE tag combined with a downward trend already forced a mandatory step-down to T2, but raw_avg 3PM of 3.2 with volatile distribution means the floor at T2 is pattern-based rather than structural — a cold-shooting night (well within normal variance for a volatile 3PM shooter) produces a miss, and there is no iron_floor protection.

min_floor of 15.8 with avg minutes of only 19.5 means White's PTS floor is highly minutes-contingent — proj_min=22 failed Override 1 (requires ≥30) yet the pick was kept, and post-miss bounce-back is only 60% (lift=0.76). A single foul trouble or game-script adjustment easily drops him to 15-17 minutes, well below what's needed to clear PTS T10 reliably.

Same structural minutes risk as the PTS pick — min_floor of 15.8 and avg minutes of 19.5 make the 3PM floor entirely contingent on White getting meaningful run; any game-script reduction or foul trouble collapses the opportunity base for even a T1 3PM threshold.

min_floor of 17.9 with avg minutes of 29.8 signals extreme minutes volatility — the gap between floor and average is the largest on the slate, and VOLATILE tag plus FG_MARGIN_THIN (1%, the most severe thin margin present) means Bane is one cold night or reduced-minutes game away from missing even this stepped-down T10 threshold; typical miss is 6.0 pts below, the highest miss depth on the slate.

VOLATILE on a cold-momentum team (team_momentum=cold) with a tough defense and min_floor=25.0 averaging only 28.3 minutes — the narrow average-to-floor gap means any minute compression under the cold team context pushes Curry's volatile PTS distribution toward a miss; post-miss bounce-back is only 67% and the typical miss is 5.5 pts below, indicating meaningful downside when he misses.

Per historical lessons, iron_floor AST props on non-primary-playmakers (even with high raw_avg_AST=5.7) carry structural miss risk when the player's game concentrates on scoring — Booker is primarily a scorer, and in a competitive game where PHX may rely on him for buckets, assist accumulation to reach T4 (a relatively high AST threshold) is role-dependent rather than guaranteed even with iron_floor protection.

min_floor=0.0 is a critical flag — the system notes this as CRITICAL and proj_min is only 21.1 average, meaning Green's floor is entirely undefined and a single DNP, rest decision, or early exit produces a total miss; VOLATILE tag compounds this with a wide scoring distribution, and typical miss depth is 6.0 pts below threshold — the structural floor is absent.
